metadata.title: Pau Bartrina | Senior Frontend Engineer
metadata.description: Senior Frontend Engineer with 15+ years of experience in Angular, TypeScript, NgRx, and Nx. Specializing in modular architecture, testing, and technical leadership.

hero.title: Senior Frontend Engineer with 15+ years of experience
hero.subtitle: in the Angular ecosystem, modular architecture, and technical leadership

about.heading: About Me
about.bio: Senior Frontend Engineer and Tech Lead with over 15 years of experience, specializing in the Angular ecosystem (NgRx, Nx, TypeScript). Expert in codebase modernization and implementing testing cultures with Vitest and Playwright. A collaborative leader dedicated to connecting design, product, and engineering to deliver flawless user experiences.

jobs.description: Frontend leadership for the launch of a new strategic product. Angular SME, improving performance metrics by 15%. Testing with Vitest and Playwright.
Scalable web architecture with Angular, Nx, and NgRx. Co-designed a high-performance dynamic forms system. Testing with Jest and Cypress.
Prototyping and evaluation of frontend frameworks (Angular 2, NativeScript, React Native, Ionic 2) for enterprise applications. PoCs presented at FinTech conferences.
Development of secure and efficient frontends with JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3, focusing on encryption-sensitive applications.
Co-founded a digital platform startup. Frontend development and collaboration on UI/UX design and strategic planning.

items.title: paubartrina.cat
items.description: Personal website and blog built with Next.js 16, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS v4. Includes multi-language support, MDX blog, and automatic deployment to Vercel.

items.title: Banking Management System
items.description: Angular 17+ dashboard for banking product management with NgRx, modular architecture, and 90% test coverage.

items.title: Monitoring Dashboard
items.description: Real-time data visualization interface with Angular, D3.js, and WebSockets for server infrastructure.

items.title: IoT Mobile App
items.description: NativeScript application for IoT device control with OAuth2 authentication and offline synchronization.

uses.title: Uses
uses.description: My development setup — editor, terminal, hardware, and applications.
uses.heading: Uses
uses.subtitle: My development setup and the tools I use daily.
uses.intro: Inspired by <link>uses.tech</link>, here's a list of the tools I use regularly.

editor.heading: Editor
items: Webstorm as my main editor with the Islands Dark theme
Font: JetBrains Mono with ligatures enabled
Extensions: ESLint, Prettier, Angular Language Service, GitLens
Vim keybindings with the VSCodeVim extension
Settings Sync enabled to keep configuration across machines

terminal.heading: Terminal
items: iTerm2 with Oh My Zsh and the Powerlevel10k theme
tmux for session and pane management
Custom aliases for Git, npm, and quick navigation
fzf for fuzzy file and history search
bat, exa, and ripgrep as modern alternatives to cat, ls, and grep

browser.heading: Browser & DevTools
items: Brave Browser as my main browser
Angular DevTools for debugging Angular applications
React Developer Tools when working with React
Lighthouse and Web Vitals for performance audits

hardware.heading: Hardware
items: Keychron K15 Pro mechanical keyboard with Brown switches

desktop.heading: Desktop Apps
items: Raycast as launcher and clipboard manager
Notion for notes and personal documentation

uses.footer: Inspired by <link>uses.tech</link>, a directory of developer /uses pages.

ara.title: Now
ara.description: What I'm doing now - my now page
ara.heading: What I'm doing now
ara.location: I live in Sant Pere de Vilamajor, at the foot of Montseny.
ara.occupation: I'm a front-end web developer and currently looking for work.
priorities: Due to personal whims and "strategic" decisions, the company I was working at has dissolved and I'm now unemployed — that said, I only have gratitude towards the company where I spent nearly 8 years.
Time to get going with the website built with Nuxt that I had on stand-by.
Sven had a dental cleaning and they pulled one of his teeth (he was never the wise type).
We had a bit of drama with some water leaks this rainy month, but everything is under control now.
excitement: I'm on the home stretch of the book I started! I'm really enjoying it and will probably continue with the Stormlight Archive saga.
The bonsai trees are starting to sprout vigorously — now comes a beautiful season of pruning, repotting, and training.
ara.nowPageDescription: This is a <link>/now page</link>, inspired by Derek Sivers' movement.
ara.footer: This is my "now" page inspired by the <link>now page</link> movement.
